title: Disable Editing and Reposition SFDC Object Name Column
story: As a DataTools Pro user, I want the SFDC Object Name column to be non-editable and positioned as the first column in the scorecard, so that the Salesforce-sourced data remains accurate and the most identifying column is immediately visible.
description:
Two changes to the SFDC Object Name column in the scorecard table:

1. **Disable editing** — The Salesforce Sink Object Name field should be read-only for end users. This data comes directly from the Salesforce API and must not be manually altered.
2. **Reposition as first column** — Move the SFDC Object Name column to the left-most position in the table for better visibility and context.
acceptance_criteria: SFDC Object Name column is not editable by end users
Attempting to edit the SFDC Object Name cell does not open an editor
SFDC Object Name is displayed as the first (left-most) column in the scorecard table
Column reordering does not affect the locked position of the SFDC Object Name column
ux_changes: SFDC Object Name column is made read-only and repositioned as the first column in the scorecard table. Visual styling indicates the column is non-editable.
implementation_notes: Relates to MBT-7 (Split Object Name / Label) — the non-editable field should display the concatenated Label (API_Name) format while the underlying data uses the split fields.
